summary: Phase 4 backend testing of Super Master Admin Console additions. 18/18 tests passed in /app/backend/tests/test_admin_phase4.py covering finance intelligence (30/90/365-day windows), user-growth series, usage heatmap (7x24 grid), live provider health, infrastructure (psutil/db/redis/latency), per-user usage analytics, login alerts, super-admin impersonation (200 with audit row + 403 for plain admin), public feature-flags endpoint (15 defaults, anon + authed evaluation, disable->journal:false propagation, restore->true), LIVE AI MODEL ROUTING (POST /admin/ai/feature-models chat=nova-pro-v1:0 -> 17s cache wait -> POST /api/chat -> /admin/ai/usage.by_model contains amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 with non-zero tokens and cost_usd, by_feature contains 'chat'; restored to nova-lite after), and regression on /api/expo-qr, /api/auth/otp/request, /api/chat (persisted to chat_messages).

minor.issue: by_model rows return combined 'tokens' field but not separate input_tokens/output_tokens, although the underlying admin_ai_usage docs store both. Consider exposing input_tokens/output_tokens separately in the by_model aggregation for finer dashboards.

MOCKED — storage_cost is dbStats.storageSize x $0.10/GB/mo and voice_cost is phone_calls.duration_sec x $0.18/min. Not real Stripe/telco-billed cost. Revenue is plan_price x user_count, not Stripe revenue. UI must display 'estimated' badge.

Bedrock 'healthy' state is derived from any HTTP <500 response of bedrock-runtime root URL; gives false-positive 'healthy' even when no valid bearer token. Functional for liveness only, not auth/quota.

action_items: (Optional) Expose input_tokens / output_tokens separately in /admin/ai/usage by_model aggregation.
(Optional) Add an 'estimated' badge in Finance Intelligence UI given storage/voice/revenue are derived, not billed.
(Optional) Tighten /admin/health/providers-live to call a model-listing endpoint instead of the bare bedrock-runtime root (currently returns 404 yet is reported healthy because <500).
critical_code_review_comments: admin_routes.py now 1614 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting per domain (finance.py / metrics.py / health.py / users.py / public.py).

_MODEL_CACHE is a module-level dict in server.py with no eviction; fine for one worker but stale across uvicorn workers. If scaled, switch to per-request DB lookup or shared Redis cache (Redis is already detected in /infrastructure).

_bedrock_converse passes user_id=None for /api/chat because chat handler does not read request.state.user_id. The auth middleware DOES populate it — propagate to _bedrock_converse so per-user analytics (counts.messages already covered, but ai.cost_usd in /admin/users/{id}/usage will be empty for chat calls until this is wired).

finance_intelligence iterates db.users.find for plan_distribution and runs 30/90/365 day-by-day aggregates in Python — O(days) round trips. For 365-day windows this is 366 Mongo queries per call; collapse into one $group with date buckets.

public /features/public endpoint uses request.client.host as anon uid; behind a proxy/ingress this is the proxy IP, so all anon users share the same rollout bucket. Use X-Forwarded-For first hop or generate a stable cookie.

impersonate() issues tokens with full role/plan of target but does not embed an 'impersonated_by' claim in the JWT. The token is therefore indistinguishable from a real session — if the operator's machine is compromised, audit trail is the only forensic. Add an 'impersonated_by' JWT claim and surface it in /api/auth/me.
test_credentials: admin@oraos.app / Admin@123456 (super_admin) from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. End-user JWTs minted in-test via auth_mod.upsert_oauth_user + auth_mod.issue_tokens (same pattern as iteration 10).
seed_data_creation: Created in this run: 2 TEST_p4_* users (one end-user, one admin role) via auth_mod.upsert_oauth_user; toggled 'journal' feature flag to disabled then restored to enabled; switched admin_feature_models[chat] primary_model_id to amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 then restored to amazon.nova-lite-v1:0; wrote ~2 admin_ai_usage rows from the live chat call; created 1 audit row 'user.impersonated'; created TEST_p4_reg_* OTP entries. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 4 endpoints are green AND the LIVE routing pipeline is verified end-to-end: changing admin_feature_models[chat].primary_model_id is observable in /api/chat within ~16s (cache TTL=15s). Note _bedrock_converse currently passes user_id=None from /api/chat — per-user AI cost in /admin/users/{id}/usage will therefore not attribute chat tokens to the calling user even though chat_messages.count is incremented. If next iteration adds per-user cost UI, main agent must wire request.state.user_id into chat()->_bedrock_converse(user_id=...). Frontend feature gating in /app/frontend/src/features.tsx was NOT exercised here — only the public endpoint contract was. The full prior suite (test_admin_console.py) was not re-run in this iteration; if regression is needed, run both files together.
rca of the issue: n/a — no failing tests after fixing a self-inflicted assertion in test_route_switch_takes_effect (was checking m.input_tokens+m.output_tokens but the by_model aggregation returns a single 'tokens' field). Switched the assertion to read 'tokens' which is what the API actually returns. No production code changed.
